cue: How do you wave off someone's thanks?

es.meaning: Just imagine; wow — an intensifier of surprise or empathy, NOT a reply to thanks.

it.meaning: Don't mention it; not at all; also 'of course not' — the standard wave-off of thanks.

bridge.note: A false friend built from the same instruction: both tell you to picture something. Italian's figurati has drifted into courtesy — picture how little trouble it was — and become the standard reply to thanks. Spanish imagínate stayed an amazement marker: picture how big it was. Same imperative, opposite directions: one shrinks the favour, the other inflates the story.

interference.es_to_it: Answering 'gracias' with 'imagínate' confuses a Mexican — the wave-off is 'no hay de qué' or 'de nada'. Save imagínate for astonishment.
interference.it_to_es: Replying to grazie with a literal translation fails; figurati (or figurati!) is the ritual. And when an Italian says figurati to decline your apology, no imagining is requested.

review.notes: From Joe's Italian Matters marker list 2026-07-20 (discovery source only; all examples freshly authored). ES pairing by Claude, UNVERIFIED. The thanks-ritual asymmetry pairs nicely with 0039's crepi ritual — possible themed sequence on scripted replies.
